title: eriophorum angustifolium subsp. triste
intro: Eriophorum angustifolium subsp. triste, also known as cotton grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. The roots of Eriophorum angustifolium subsp. triste contain iridoids and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal and health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions.
